column2: Retrieve the names of all customers who are from the city 'Mumbai'.
column5: `select customer_name from customers where city='Mumbai';`
column6: `SELECT customer_name FROM customers WHERE city = 'Mumbai';`

column2: Get the product names and prices of products where the price is greater than 500.
column5: select product_name from products where price>500;
column6: SELECT product_name, `price` FROM products WHERE price > 500;

column2: List all orders placed after '2023-01-01'.
column5: `select * from orders where order_date>'2023-01-01';`
column6: `SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date > '2023-01-01';`

column2: Display the top 5 most expensive products based on price.
column5: select product_name from products order by `desc` limit 5;
column6: SELECT product_name, `price` FROM products ORDER BY `price` DESC LIMIT 5;

column2: Find the total quantity sold for each product.
column5: `select p.product_name, sum(o.quantity) as total_qty_sold from products p join order_items o on p.product_id=o.product_id group by p.product_name;`
column6: `SELECT p.product_name, SUM(o.quantity) AS total_qty_sold FROM products p JOIN order_items o ON p.product_id = o.product_id GROUP BY p.product_name;`

column2: Retrieve the customer names along with their total order amount.
column5: select c.customer_name, sum(o.total_amount) as tot_ord_amt from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS tot_ord_amt FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id `GROUP` `BY` `c.customer_name`;

column2: Find all products that belong to the category 'Electronics' and have a price less than 1000.
column5: `select * from products where category='Electronics' and price<1000;`
column6: `SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics' AND price < 1000;`

column2: List the order IDs and total number of items (quantity sum) in each order.
column5: select order_id, sum(`total_amount`) from `orders` group by order_id;
column6: SELECT order_id, SUM(`quantity`) FROM `order_items` GROUP BY order_id;

column2: Display the customers who have not placed any orders.
column5: select c.customer_name, `o.order_id` from customers c left join orders o `where` o.order_id is null;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o `ON` c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;

column2: Get the product name and total revenue (quantity × item_price) generated for each product.
column5: select p.product_name, sum(o.quantity)`*``o.item_price` as tot_revenue from products p join order_items o on p.product_id=`o.order_items` group by p.product_name;
column6: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(o.quantity `*` o.item_price) AS tot_revenue FROM products p JOIN order_items o ON p.product_id = o.`product_id` GROUP BY p.product_name;

column2: Retrieve the top 3 customers based on total order amount in descending order.
column5: select c.customer_name, `o.total_amount` from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id order by total_amount desc limit 3;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, `SUM`(o.total_amount) AS total_amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id `GROUP` `BY` c.customer_name ORDER BY total_amount DESC LIMIT 3;

column2: Find the average price of products for each category.
column5: `select category, avg(price) from products group by category;`
column6: `SELECT category, AVG(price) FROM products GROUP BY category;`

column2: List all orders along with the customer name who placed them.
column5: `select c.customer_name, o.* from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id;`
column6: `SELECT o.order_id, o.order_date, o.total_amount, c.customer_name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;`

column2: Display the products that have never been ordered.
column5: `select p.product_name from products p left join order_items oi on p.product_id=oi.product_id where oi.product_id is null;`
column6: `SELECT p.product_name FROM products p LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id WHERE oi.product_id IS NULL;`

column2: Get the total number of orders placed by each customer, sorted in descending order.
column5: select c.customer_name, count(o.order_id) as num_of_ords from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id group by c.customer_name order by count(o.order_id) `as` desc;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS num_of_ords FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name ORDER BY `num_of_ords` DESC;

column2: Retrieve the order IDs where the total quantity of items is greater than 10.
column5: `select o.order_id, sum(oi.quantity) as tot_qty from orders o join order_items oi on o.order_id=oi.order_id group by o.order_id having tot_qty>10;`
column6: `SELECT o.order_id, SUM(oi.quantity) AS tot_qty FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY o.order_id HAVING SUM(oi.quantity) > 10;`

column2: Find the total revenue generated on each order.
column5: select order_id, sum(`total_amount`) from `orders` group by order_id;
column6: SELECT order_id, SUM(`quantity` `*` `item_price`) AS total_revenue FROM `order_items` GROUP BY order_id;

column2: List the customers who signed up before '2022-01-01' and are from 'Delhi'.
column5: `select customer_name from customers where signup_date<'2022-01-01' and city='Delhi';`
column6: `SELECT customer_name FROM customers WHERE signup_date < '2022-01-01' AND city = 'Delhi';`

column2: Display the most recent order date for each customer.
column5: select c.customer_name, `o.order_date` from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=`o.order_id` order by o.order_date;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, `MAX`(o.order_date) AS recent_order FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.`customer_id` `GROUP` `BY` c.customer_name;

column2: Get the product categories along with the total quantity sold in each category.
column5: `select p.category, sum(oi.quantity) as tot_qty from products p join order_items oi on p.product_id=oi.product_id group by p.category;`
column6: `SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity) AS tot_qty FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category;`

column2: Retrieve the customers who have placed more than 5 orders.
column5: `select c.customer_name, count(o.order_id) as no_of_ords from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id group by c.customer_name having no_of_ords>5;`
column6: `SELECT c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS no_of_ords FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name HAVING COUNT(o.order_id) > 5;`

column2: Find the highest priced product in each category.
column5: select category, `price` from products group by category order by price;
column6: SELECT category, `MAX`(price) AS highest_price FROM products GROUP BY category;

column2: List the order IDs where the total revenue is between 500 and 2000.
column5: select order_id, `total_amount` from `orders` where total_amount between 500 and 2000;
column6: SELECT order_id, SUM(`quantity` `*` `item_price`) AS total_revenue FROM `order_items` GROUP BY order_id HAVING SUM(quantity * item_price) BETWEEN 500 AND 2000;

column2: Display the customer name and their first order date.
column5: select c.customer_name, `o.order_date` from `customer` c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id order by o.order_date asc limit 1;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, `MIN`(o.order_date) AS first_order FROM `customers` c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id `GROUP` `BY` c.customer_name;

column2: Get the total number of products sold (sum of quantity) for each customer.
column5: select c.customer_name, sum(oi.quantity) from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id join `order_items` on o.order_id=`oi.order_id` group by c.customer_name;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, SUM(oi.quantity) FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items `oi` ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_name;

column2: Retrieve all customer names and their cities.
column5: `select customer_name, city from customers;`
column6: `SELECT customer_name, city FROM customers;`

column2: Find all accounts with a balance greater than 50,000.
column5: `select * from accounts where balance>50000;`
column6: `SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE balance > 50000;`

column2: Display account_id and balance of all accounts sorted by balance in descending order.
column5: `select account_id, balance from accounts order by balance desc;`
column6: `SELECT account_id, balance FROM accounts ORDER BY balance DESC;`

column2: Count the number of accounts for each account_type.
column5: select `account_id`, count(account_type) as no_of_accs from accounts group by `account_id`;
column6: SELECT account_type, COUNT(`*`) AS no_of_accounts FROM accounts GROUP BY account_type;

column2: Retrieve customer_name and account_id for all customers who have accounts.
column5: `select account_id, customer_name from customers c join accounts a on c.customer_id=a.customer_id;`
column6: `SELECT c.customer_name, a.account_id FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id;`

column2: Retrieve the names of all customers along with the cities they belong to.
column5: select `*` customer_name, city from customers;
column6: SELECT customer_name, city FROM customers;

column2: Find all accounts that have a balance greater than 50,000.
column5: `select * from accounts where balance>50000;`
column6: `SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE balance > 50000;`

column2: Display each account’s identifier and its balance, sorted from highest to lowest balance.
column5: `select account_id, balance from accounts order by balance desc;`
column6: `SELECT account_id, balance FROM accounts ORDER BY balance DESC;`

column2: Count how many accounts exist for each type of account.
column5: `select account_type, count(account_id) as num_of_accs from accounts group by account_type;`
column6: `SELECT account_type, COUNT(*) AS num_of_accounts FROM accounts GROUP BY account_type;`

column2: Retrieve the names of customers along with their account identifiers for those who have accounts.
column5: `select customer_name, account_id from customers c join accounts a on c.customer_id=a.customer_id;`
column6: `SELECT c.customer_name, a.account_id FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id;`

column2: Retrieve all transactions that are of type credit.
column5: `select * from transactions where transaction_type='Credit';`
column6: `SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE transaction_type = 'Credit';`

column2: Find the total balance held by each customer.
column5: select `c.customer_name`, sum(a.balance) as tot_bal from customers c join accounts a on c.customer_id=a.customer_id group by c.customer_name;
column6: SELECT `a.customer_id`, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts a GROUP BY a.customer_id;

column2: Display each transaction’s account identifier and date, sorted by date in ascending order.
column5: select `transaction_id`, transaction_date from transactions order by transaction_date asc;
column6: SELECT `account_id`, transaction_date FROM transactions ORDER BY transaction_date ASC;

column2: Retrieve customer names along with their account balances for those having balance greater than 10,000.
column5: `select c.customer_name, a.balance from customers c join accounts a on c.customer_id=a.customer_id where balance>10000;`
column6: `SELECT c.customer_name, a.balance FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id WHERE a.balance > 10000;`

column2: Count how many transactions exist for each account, but only include those accounts having more than 3 transactions.
column5: select `account_id`, count(transaction_id) as `transctions_count` from accounts a join transactions t on a.account_id=t.account_id having transctions_count>3;
column6: SELECT t.account_id, COUNT(`*`) AS `transaction_count` FROM transactions t `GROUP` `BY` t.account_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 3;

column2: Retrieve the names of all customers who are from Mumbai.
column5: `select customer_name from customers where city='Mumbai';`
column6: `SELECT customer_name FROM customers WHERE city = 'Mumbai';`

column2: Show the total number of orders placed by each customer.
column5: select c.customer_name, `sum`(o.order_id) as tot_orders from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id group by c.customer_name;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, `COUNT`(o.order_id) FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name;

column2: List all products along with their prices, sorted from highest to lowest price.
column5: `select product_name, price from products order by price desc;`
column6: `SELECT product_name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;`

column2: Find the names of customers who have placed at least one order.
column5: select c.customer_name from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id;
column6: SELECT `DISTINCT` c.customer_name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;

column2: Display the top 3 orders with the highest total amount.
column5: `select order_id, total_amount from orders order by total_amount desc limit 3;`
column6: `SELECT order_id, total_amount FROM orders ORDER BY total_amount DESC LIMIT 3;`

column2: Find the total revenue generated from each product.
column5: `select p.product_name, sum(oi.quantity*oi.item_price) as tot_revenue from products p join order_items oi on p.product_id=oi.product_id group by p.product_name;`
column6: `SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.item_price) FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.product_name;`

column2: List the names of customers who have never placed any orders.
column5: select c.customer_name from customers c `join` orders o on c.customer_id=o.customer_id where o.order_id IS NULL;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name FROM customers c `LEFT` JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;

column2: Show the average order amount for each customer, but only include those whose average is above 1000.
column5: select c.customer_name, avg(o.total_amount) as avg_amt from customers c join orders o on c.customer_id=`o.order_id` group by c.customer_name having `avg_amount`>1000;
column6: SELECT c.customer_name, AVG(o.total_amount) FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = `o.customer_id` GROUP BY c.customer_name HAVING `AVG`(o.total_amount) > 1000;

column2: Retrieve all orders placed in the year 2023, sorted by date in ascending order.
column5: `select * from orders where year(order_date)='2023' order by order_date asc;`
column6: `SELECT * FROM orders WHERE YEAR(order_date) = 2023 ORDER BY order_date ASC;`

column2: Display the product names and total quantity sold for each product, sorted by total quantity in descending order.
column5: select p.product_name, sum(oi.quantity) as `tot_qty_sold` from products p join order_items oi on p.product_id=oi.product_id group by p.product_name order by `tot_qty` desc;
column6: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS `tot_qty` FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.product_name ORDER BY tot_qty DESC;
